CGAL::Qt::Converter.name: CGAL::Qt::Converter
CGAL::Qt::Converter.brief: An object of type Converter converts 2D CGAL Kernel objects to their equivalent objects in Qt , and the other way round.
CGAL::Qt::Converter.detailed: Note that some objects have no equivalent. For example the CGAL::Circle_2<K> cannot be converted to something in Qt , and the unbounded objects CGAL::Ray_2<K> and CGAL::Line_2<K> are clipped. Note also that CGAL and Qt sometimes also use the same word for different things. For example line denotes an unbounded line in CGAL, whereas it denotes a bounded segment in Qt . K must be a model of Kernel .

members.name: Converter
members.brief: The clipping rect is used for unbounded CGAL objects.

CGAL::Qt::GraphicsItem.name: CGAL::Qt::GraphicsItem
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsItem.brief: The CGAL graphics items hold pointers to CGAL data structures.
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsItem.detailed: When a data structure changes, a signal is emitted. An object derived from type GraphicsItem must provide an implementation of the virtual slot modelChanged() . This typically triggers redrawing, as well as recomputation of the bounding box which in turn may trigger redrawing of overlapping graphics items.

members.name: modelChanged
members.brief: This slot must be provided by derived classes.

CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircleInput.name: CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircleInput
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircleInput.brief: An object of type GraphicsViewCircleInput creates a circle, defined by either center and radius, or two or three points on the circle.
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircleInput.detailed: A new vertex is inserted every time the left mouse button is released. The Del key removes the last entered point. The Esc key removes all entered points. K must be a model of Kernel .

members.name: GraphicsViewCircleInput
members.detailed: p is a parent object. s is the scene where the circle is generated. pointsOnCircle is the number of points on the circle to be generated, that is the default value 1 corresponds to the case center/radius.

members.name: generate
members.brief: The object o contains a std::pair<K::Point_2, K::FT> for center and radius, or a std::pair<K::Point_2, K::Point_2> for two points defining the circle, or CGAL::array<K::Point_2, 3> for three points defining the circle.

CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircularArcInput.name: CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircularArcInput
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircularArcInput.brief: An object of type GraphicsViewCircularArcInput creates a circular arc, defined by three points on a circle.
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewCircularArcInput.detailed: A new vertex is inserted every time the left mouse button is released. The Del key removes the last entered point. The Esc key removes all entered points. K must be a model of CircularKernel .

members.name: GraphicsViewCircularArcInput
members.detailed: p is a parent object. s is the scene where the circular arc is generated.

members.name: generate
members.brief: The object o contains a Circular_arc_2<K> .

CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewNavigation.name: CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewNavigation
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewNavigation.brief: An object of type GraphicsViewNavigation can be added as event filter to a Qt::QGraphicsView and its viewport.
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewNavigation.detailed: Dragging the left mouse button while holding the 'Ctrl' key defines a zoom rectangle. Dragging the right mouse button while holding the 'Ctrl' key translates the scene. 'Ctrl-Shift' and a click of the right mouse button translates what is under the mouse to the center.

members.name: eventFilter
members.brief: The event filter.

members.name: mouseCoordinates
members.brief: Emits the real world mouse coordinates.

CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewPolylineInput.name: CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewPolylineInput
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewPolylineInput.brief: An object of type GraphicsViewPolylineInput creates a list of points.
CGAL::Qt::GraphicsViewPolylineInput.detailed: A new vertex is inserted every time the left mouse button is pressed. The list of points is emitted on a right click or when the number of points specified in the constructor is reached. You can use the 'Del' or 'Backspace' key if you want to remove your last entered point in the polygon, and the 'Esc' key if you want to remove all points. The tool can serve at the same time for entering a single point, a polyline with a given number of points, and for open as well as closed polylines. For polylines the segment between the last entered point and the current mouse position is only drawn correctly when mouse tracking is enabled in the graphics view. The same holds for closed polygons. K must be a model of Kernel .

members.name: GraphicsViewPolylineInput
members.detailed: p is a parent object. s is the scene where the polyline is generated. n is the number of points of the polyline to be generated. If n = 0 , that is the default value, the number of points of the polyline is not limited. closed determines if a closed polygon is drawn during the input.

members.name: generate
members.brief: The object o contains a std::list<K::Point_2> .

CGAL::Qt::PainterOstream.name: CGAL::Qt::PainterOstream
CGAL::Qt::PainterOstream.brief: An object of type PainterOstream provides output operators for Kernel objects.
CGAL::Qt::PainterOstream.detailed: As CGAL has unbounded objects the PainterOstream must be constructed with a clipping rectangle. This is typically the visible area of the widget where the unbounded object is displayed.

members.name: PainterOstream
members.brief: The clipping rect is used for unbounded Kernel objects.

PkgGraphicsViewRef.name: PkgGraphicsViewRef
PkgGraphicsViewRef.detailed: Andreas Fabri and Laurent Rineau This package provides classes for displaying CGAL objects and data structures in the Qt 6 Graphics View Framework . Introduced in: CGAL 3.4 Depends on: Qt 6 BibTeX: cgal:fr-cqgvf-26a License: GPL This package provides some classes which allow to use CGAL classes in Qt applications which make use of the Qt Graphics View Framework .
